questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct preposition: 'The ore sample was analysed ____ traces of gold.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Analyse for' is the idiomatic collocation when testing for the presence of something.

**Working.** We test a sample 'for' a substance; the other prepositions do not fit this sense.

**Answer.** for.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A is three times as old as B, and the sum of their ages is $48$ years. A's age is _____ years.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Express both ages in one variable.

**Working.** $A=3B$ and $A+B=48\Rightarrow 4B=48\Rightarrow B=12,\;A=36$.

**Answer.** $36$ years.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the word most similar in meaning to 'ABATE'.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Abate' means to become less intense or to reduce.

**Working.** Among the options, 'diminish' is the closest synonym; 'increase' is its antonym.

**Answer.** diminish.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Compute the average of the four bar values shown: $4, 6, 8, 10$.

figure.caption: Bar chart of four values
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='45' y='90' width='25' height='30'/><rect x='85' y='75' width='25' height='45'/><rect x='125' y='60' width='25' height='60'/><rect x='165' y='45' width='25' height='75'/></g><text x='55' y='138' font-size='8'>4, 6, 8, 10</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average $=\dfrac{\sum x}{n}$.

**Working.**
$$\bar{x}=\frac{4+6+8+10}{4}=\frac{28}{4}=7$$

**Answer.** $7$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: MINER : ORE :: FARMER : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'worker : product extracted/produced'.

**Working.** A miner extracts ore; a farmer produces a crop. A tractor and field are tools/locations, not products.

**Answer.** crop.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An article marked $25\%$ above its cost price is sold at a $10\%$ discount. The profit percentage is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply markup then discount on a base cost of $100$.

**Working.** CP $=100$, MP $=125$, SP $=125\times0.9=112.5$; profit $=12.5\%$.

**Answer.** $12.5\%$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Five years ago, a man was seven times as old as his son. The man is now $40$ years old. The son's present age is _____ years.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Translate the past condition into an equation.

**Working.** Five years ago the man was $35$, so $35=7(\text{son}-5)\Rightarrow \text{son}-5=5\Rightarrow \text{son}=10$.

**Answer.** $10$ years.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The length of the diagonal of a rectangle of sides $8$ and $6$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The diagonal is the hypotenuse of a right triangle with the two sides as legs.

**Working.** $\sqrt{8^2+6^2}=\sqrt{100}=10$.

**Answer.** $10$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Using A=1, B=2, ..., Z=26, the sum of the letter values of the word 'ORE' is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Replace each letter with its position value and add.

**Working.** O$=15$, R$=18$, E$=5$; sum $=38$.

**Answer.** $38$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The remainder when $2^{10}$ is divided by $7$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Use $2^3=8\equiv 1 \pmod 7$.

**Working.** $2^{10}=(2^3)^3\cdot 2 \equiv 1^3\cdot 2 = 2 \pmod 7$.

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Read the loose and compacted densities from the bar chart. The volume reduction $(1-\rho_1/\rho_2)$ is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="132.9" width="50" height="17.1" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="128.9" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">100</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">loose ρ₁</text><rect x="180" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">700</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">compacted ρ₂</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kg/m³</text></svg>
figure.caption: Loose and compacted densities shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** VR = (1 − ρ₁/ρ₂)×100, both densities read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-\dfrac{100}{700}\right)\times100=85.7\%.$$

**Answer.** $85.7\%$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $6\,\text{t}$ of a greenhouse gas. Reading the gas's GWP₁₀₀ from the figure, its CO₂-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="36" width="220" height="56" rx="6" fill="rgba(217,119,6,0.12)" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="58" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">the gas</text><text x="150" y="80" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">GWP₁₀₀ = 25</text><text x="150" y="112" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂-e = mass × GWP</text></svg>
figure.caption: Global-warming potential of the gas marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO₂-e = mass × GWP, with the GWP read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$6\times25=150.$$

**Answer.** $150$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: For an ideal gas at 25 °C and 1 atm, converting a gaseous pollutant from ppm(v) to mg/m³ requires multiplying by
questions.solution:
**Concept.** mg/m³ = ppm × MW / 24.45 (molar volume 24.45 L at 25°C, 1 atm).

**Working.**
× MW/24.45.

**Answer.** MW/24.45

figure.caption: Sequential stages of the EIA process.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The EMP details mitigation, monitoring and institutional arrangements.

**Working.**
EMP follows impact prediction.

**Answer.** EMP

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays by first-order kinetics with rate constant $k=0.231\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Its half-life is _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For first order, $t_{1/2}=\dfrac{\ln 2}{k}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.693}{0.231}=3.$$

**Answer.** $3\,\text{d}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: The main purpose of maintaining ~850–1100 °C with adequate residence time in an MSW incinerator is to
options: ensure complete combustion and destroy dioxins/pathogens
reduce the need for air pollution control
questions.solution:
**Concept.** High temperature + 2 s residence ensures complete burnout and dioxin destruction.

**Working.**
Complete combustion.

**Answer.** Complete combustion

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: First-order decay, $k=0.05\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Half-life is _____ days.

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Wet MSW $1000\,\text{kg}$ at $60\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dry = wet × (1 − moisture).

**Working.**
$$1000(1-60/100)=400.$$

**Answer.** $400\,\text{kg}$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: Bacteria grow exponentially with specific growth rate $\mu=0.35\,\text{h}^{-1}$. The doubling (generation) time is _____ hours.

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: In the energy pyramid shown, only the producer (trophic-1) energy is marked; 10% transfers per level. Energy at trophic level $3$ is _____ kcal/m²/yr.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Eₙ = E₀(0.1)ⁿ, with E₀ read from the pyramid.

**Working.**
$$15000\times0.1^{2}=150.$$

**Answer.** $150$

questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 200" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="46" y2="170" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="300" y2="20" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">depth ↓</text><text x="252" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="270" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="270" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="46" y1="150" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">t = 300 s</text><text x="118" y="165" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">settles 2.5 m</text></svg>

**Concept.** v_s = depth ÷ time (read both from the trajectory).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{2.5\,\text{m}}{300\,\text{s}}\times1000=8.33\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $8.33\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: The nutrient most commonly limiting and therefore triggering eutrophication in freshwater lakes is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Phosphorus is usually the limiting nutrient in freshwater; its loading drives algal blooms.

**Working.**
Phosphorus.

**Answer.** Phosphorus

Bicarbonate dominates.

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A direct consequence of eutrophication in a water body is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Algal blooms die and decompose, consuming DO and raising BOD.

**Working.**
DO depletion.

**Answer.** Oxygen depletion

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: The optimum carbon-to-nitrogen (C/N) ratio for efficient aerobic composting is approximately
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A C/N around 25–30:1 balances microbial energy (C) and protein synthesis (N).

**Working.**
≈ 25–30:1.

**Answer.** 25–30:1

questions.solution:

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Which characteristics define a hazardous waste under typical regulatory frameworks?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The four hazard characteristics are ignitability, corrosivity, reactivity and toxicity; moisture content is not one.

**Working.**
Ignitability, corrosivity, reactivity.

**Answer.** Ignitability, corrosivity, reactivity

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: A 'cradle-to-grave' Life Cycle Assessment evaluates environmental burdens from
options: raw-material extraction through to final disposal
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Cradle-to-grave LCA spans extraction → production → use → end-of-life.

**Working.**
Extraction to disposal.

**Answer.** Raw material to disposal

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: Read the Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations from the bar chart. The total hardness as CaCO₃ (eq. wt Ca 20, Mg 12.15) is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">20</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Ca²⁺</text><rect x="180" y="42" width="50" height="108" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="38" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">18</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">Mg²⁺</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ concentrations shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Convert each ion to CaCO₃ (×50/eq.wt) and sum, with both concentrations read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$20\times\dfrac{50}{20}+18\times\dfrac{50}{12.15}=124.1.$$

**Answer.** $124.1$

questions.stem: A population grows exponentially at $r=0.08\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the initial size $N_0$ from the figure, after $14$ yr, $N=$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M40 150 C120 145 180 120 230 80 C260 55 285 35 300 25" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="40" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="46" y="146" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">N₀ = 500</text><text x="14" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="278" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial population N₀ marked on the curve at t = 0. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** N = N₀e^{rt}, with N₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$500e^{0.08\times14}=1532.$$

**Answer.** $1532$

questions.stem: At a discount rate of $8\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹1,20,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 6</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{120000}{(1+0.08)^{6}}=75620.$$

**Answer.** $₹75620$

questions.solution:
**Concept.** F/M = QS₀/(VX).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{12000\times250}{2000\times3000}=0.5.$$

**Concept.** Σ massᵢ × GWPᵢ, with the three masses read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$80+2\times28+0.5\times265=268.5.$$

**Answer.** $268.5$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: A stack emits $Q=80\,\text{g/s}$. Using the effective height $H$, wind speed $u$ and dispersion coefficients $\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ marked on the diagram, the ground-level centreline concentration (full reflection) is _____ ×10⁻⁴ g/m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 210" width="100%" style="max-width:380px;height:auto"><line x1="20" y1="175" x2="350" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><rect x="34" y="75" width="14" height="100" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="41" y="191" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">stack</text><path d="M48 75 Q160 64 350 44 M48 75 Q160 86 350 116" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.10)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="41" y1="75" x2="41" y2="175" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="54" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">H = 50 m</text><path d="M118 148 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="142" y="151" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">u = 6 m/s</text><text x="206" y="40" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">σ_y = 30 m,  σ_z = 15 m</text><text x="318" y="171" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">x →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Elevated point source: H, u, σ_y and σ_z are marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = Q/(πuσ_yσ_z)·exp(−H²/2σ_z²); H,u,σ_y,σ_z read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{80}{\pi\times6\times30\times15}e^{-50^2/(2\times15^2)}=0.00003646.$$

**Answer.** $0.365\times10^{-4}$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=150\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.6</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 60</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.6\times150}{60+150}=0.429.$$

**Answer.** $0.429$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: Which species are associated with photochemical (Los-Angeles type) smog?
options: Sulphur dioxide as the chief agent
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Photochemical smog is an oxidising NOₓ/VOC–sunlight system producing O₃ and PAN; SO₂ characterises reducing/London smog.

**Working.**
O₃, PAN, NOₓ.

**Answer.** Ozone, PAN and NOₓ

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 195" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="18" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="305" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="16" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">pH</text><text x="220" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">titrant volume (mL) →</text><path d="M40 150 C120 146 160 140 168 92 C176 44 230 36 305 30" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="168" y1="30" x2="168" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="168" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="118" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">equivalence</text><text x="158" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">10</text></svg>
figure.caption: Equivalence-point titrant volume marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** At the equivalence point $N_aV_a=N_bV_b$, with $V_{eq}$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$N_a=\dfrac{0.1\times10}{25}=0.04.$$

**Answer.** $0.04\,\text{N}$

questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=100\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.5</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 20</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.5\times100}{20+100}=0.417.$$

**Answer.** $0.417$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Wet MSW $1500\,\text{kg}$ at $60\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dry = wet × (1 − moisture).

**Working.**
$$1500(1-60/100)=600.$$

**Answer.** $600\,\text{kg}$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: Doubling atmospheric CO₂ adds a radiative forcing of $3.7\,\text{W/m}^2$. For a climate-sensitivity parameter of $0.8\,\text{K per W/m}^2$, the equilibrium surface warming is _____ K.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Equilibrium warming $\Delta T=\lambda\,\Delta F$ (climate sensitivity × forcing) — GHGs absorb the outgoing long-wave IR.

**Working.**
$$0.8\times3.7=2.96\,\text{K}.$$

**Answer.** $2.96\,\text{K}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows exponentially at $r=0.02\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the initial size $N_0$ from the figure, after $8$ yr, $N=$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M40 150 C120 145 180 120 230 80 C260 55 285 35 300 25" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="40" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="46" y="146" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">N₀ = 1000</text><text x="14" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="278" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial population N₀ marked on the curve at t = 0. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** N = N₀e^{rt}, with N₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$1000e^{0.02\times8}=1174.$$

**Answer.** $1174$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays first-order with $k=0.3\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Reading the initial concentration $C_0$ from the figure, its concentration after $7$ d is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 170" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="120" y="110" width="60" height="40" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.25" stroke="#1e293b"/><rect x="120" y="30" width="60" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t = 0</text><text x="150" y="96" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">C₀ = 80</text><text x="150" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial concentration C₀ marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = C₀e^{−kt}, with C₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$80e^{-0.3\times7}=9.8.$$

**Answer.** $9.8$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: $25\,\text{mL}$ of an acid is titrated with $0.05\,\text{N}$ NaOH. Reading the equivalence-point volume $V_{eq}$ from the titration curve shown, the normality of the acid is _____ N.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 195" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="18" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="305" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="16" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">pH</text><text x="220" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">titrant volume (mL) →</text><path d="M40 150 C120 146 160 140 168 92 C176 44 230 36 305 30" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="168" y1="30" x2="168" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="168" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="118" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">equivalence</text><text x="158" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">15</text></svg>
figure.caption: Equivalence-point titrant volume marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** At the equivalence point $N_aV_a=N_bV_b$, with $V_{eq}$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$N_a=\dfrac{0.05\times15}{25}=0.03.$$

**Answer.** $0.03\,\text{N}$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: Which gases are ozone-depleting substances?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** CFCs, halons and CCl₄ deplete ozone; CO₂ is a GHG but not an ODS.

**Working.**
First three.

**Answer.** CFCs, halons, CCl₄

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A facility emits $100\,\text{t}$ CO₂, $4\,\text{t}$ CH₄ (GWP 28) and $1\,\text{t}$ N₂O (GWP 265). The total carbon footprint is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 477</text><rect x="190" y="123.58490566037736" width="46" height="26.41509433962264" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="119.58490566037736" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 105</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">GHG mix (t CO₂-e)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Aggregating gases into CO₂-equivalent.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Sum each gas × its GWP.

**Working.**
$$100(1)+4(28)+1(265)=477.$$

**Answer.** $477\,\text{t CO}_2\text{-e}$
